title: dactylorhiza maculata subsp. elodes
intro: Dactylorhiza maculata subsp. elodes, also known as spotted marsh orchid, is a subspecies of orchid native to Europe and parts of Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza maculata subsp. elodes include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its antimicrobial properties which can aid in wound healing. Dactylorhiza maculata subsp. elodes can be prepared as an herbal tea or infusion, or used as a tincture, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
